visa_free.name: Iran (ordinary passport, once per 6 months, max 14 days)

outbound_official.notes: Source: Mauritania Ministry of Foreign Affairs, Cooperation and Mauritanians Abroad (diplomatie.gov.mr), page titled 'Visa' — section B lists countries with reciprocal visa suppression agreements ('accord de suppression réciproque de visa'). Algeria, Tunisia, Libya, Mali, Senegal, Gambia, Niger, Côte d'Ivoire, Guinea-Bissau, Chad, and Burkina Faso are listed without any passport-type restriction, implying they apply to ordinary passports. Morocco, Syria, Yemen, UAE, Romania, Spain, Turkey, Brazil, China, and India are explicitly limited to diplomatic/service passports and are NOT included here as ordinary-passport visa-free destinations. Iran has a special provision: all Mauritanian citizens may enter visa-free once every six months for a duration not exceeding two weeks (non-extendable). No date is published on the page. Page cached as of 2026-06-13.

e_visa.notes: From 5 January 2025, Mauritania replaced visa-on-arrival with an e-visa system. All visa-required nationalities must apply online before travel at anrpts.gov.mr. Available durations: 30, 90, 360, or 720 days. Fees for 30-day visa: Arab and African nationals — approx. €41; Rest of World — €55/$60. Fees are paid on arrival in cash (exact change required; payment is NOT made online). The visa approval carries a QR code and must be printed. Biometric data is collected at the entry point. As of June 2025, Mauritanian embassies no longer issue sticker visas — e-visa is the only route.

visa_policy.notes: Mauritania launched its e-visa system on 5 January 2025, replacing visa-on-arrival. All visa-required nationalities must obtain an e-visa at anrpts.gov.mr before travel. Fees are paid in cash on arrival (exact EUR or USD). The official MFA page (diplomatie.gov.mr/fr/node/50) lists 8 countries with full visa abolition for all passport types (Mali, Senegal, Gambia, Niger, Côte d'Ivoire, Guinea-Bissau, Chad, Burkina Faso). Algeria, Tunisia, and Libya — despite being Maghreb neighbors — are listed on the same official MFA page under diplomatic/service-passport-only agreements and have been moved to conditional_access accordingly. Max stay days for visa-free entries are not specified numerically on the official MFA page.

conditional_access.conditions: Visa abolition limited to diplomatic and service passport holders only per the official MFA bilateral agreements page. Ordinary Algerian passport holders require an e-visa.

Visa abolition limited to diplomatic and service passport holders only for entry to Mauritania. The MFA page also notes Mauritanian citizens may visit Iran visa-free once every six months for a duration not exceeding two weeks.

cbi.notes: No citizenship-by-investment program found on any official Mauritanian government source.

validation_notes: CORRECTED: Algeria, Tunisia, and Libya were previously classified as visa_free (all passports) — moved to conditional_access (diplomatic/service passports only). The official MFA page (diplomatie.gov.mr/fr/node/50) groups these three Maghreb countries under diplomatic/service-only exemptions, not under full abolition. Note: several non-official travel sources (Wikipedia, embassies.net, thingstodoinmauritania.com) list them as visa-free for ordinary passports up to 90 days; the official source takes precedence per validation methodology.
CORRECTED: e-visa available durations updated from '30, 90, or 360 days' to '30, 90, 360, or 720 days' based on multiple sources referencing the ANRPTS portal options.
CORRECTED: e-visa notes updated to clarify that fees are paid in cash on arrival (not online) — a key practical distinction confirmed by Saiga Tours guide.
CORRECTED: Removed embassyofmauritania.be from official_domains — this is a Belgian embassy third-party site, not a Mauritanian government domain.
CONFIRMED: As of June 2025, Mauritanian embassies no longer issue sticker visas; e-visa via anrpts.gov.mr is the only route for visa-required nationalities.
CONFIRMED: E-visa system launched 5 January 2025. Fee for 30-day visa: ~€41 for Arab/African nationals; €55/$60 for rest of world. Payment in exact cash on arrival.
CONFIRMED: No CBI, RBI, or fast-track programs identified on any official source.
NOTE: The 8 full-visa-abolition countries (all passports) per official MFA page are: Mali, Senegal, Gambia, Niger, Côte d'Ivoire, Guinea-Bissau, Chad, Burkina Faso.

VERIFIED 2026-06-14: Airline aggregator claim that India is visa-free or visa-on-arrival for Mauritania is FALSE. Official Mauritanian MFA page (diplomatie.gov.mr/fr/node/50) explicitly states India's exemption applies only to diplomatic and service passports ('Suppression de visa sur les passeports diplomatiques et de service'). Ordinary Indian passport holders must obtain an e-visa via anrpts.gov.mr before travel; no visa-on-arrival option exists as of January 2025. India correctly remains in conditional_access (passport_type: diplomatic/service) with e_visa required for ordinary passports.
gaps: Max stay days for the 8 visa-free (all passports) nationals not specified numerically on the official MFA page (diplomatie.gov.mr/fr/node/50); only the existence of mutual abolition agreements is confirmed. Non-official sources suggest 90 days.
ANRPTS portal (anrpts.gov.mr) returns SSL/connection errors when fetched directly; country-specific exemption list from the portal itself could not be verified in this pass.

Conflict between official MFA page and multiple non-official sources on ordinary-passport status of Algeria, Tunisia, and Libya: official MFA page groups them under diplomatic/service-only; travel sources list them as ordinary-passport visa-free. A definitive resolution requires direct confirmation from ANRPTS or an official press release.
E-visa fees for 90-day, 360-day, and 720-day durations not confirmed from any authoritative source in this pass; only 30-day pricing is well-documented.
Wikipedia mentions Egypt, Russia, and Guinea-Bissau also having diplomatic/service-only exemptions, but these are not confirmed from the official MFA page in this pass.
No information found on official sites regarding residency-by-investment (RBI), golden visa, or fast-track skilled/digital-nomad programs.
Mauritania withdrew from ECOWAS in 2000; current Arab Maghreb Union (AMU) membership is referenced but practical freedom-of-movement under AMU is largely inactive/stalled.
Dates of bilateral visa suppression agreements not published on the official MFA page; agreements are listed by country and passport type only.
